meta.desc_home: A private luxury villa — your home away from home.
meta.desc_master_suite: Master Suite — a spacious 45 m² king bedroom with private terrace and sweeping garden views.
meta.desc_bedroom_two: Bedroom Two — twin beds that convert to a king, with a work nook and garden views.
meta.desc_bedroom_three: Bedroom Three — a cosy queen room with pool views and morning light.
meta.desc_living_dining: Living & Dining — an 80 m² open-plan space that flows onto the pool terrace.

hero.tagline: A private sanctuary where luxury meets nature — your home away from home.

pool.heading: Your Private Infinity Pool
pool.p1: Drift into the day with an early morning swim, or watch the sun set from the water's edge. Our heated infinity pool blends seamlessly with the surrounding landscape, offering complete privacy and uninterrupted views.
pool.p2: The poolside terrace is furnished with sun loungers, an outdoor dining area, and a lush tropical garden — the perfect stage for lazy afternoons and long, candlelit evenings.

rooms_section.heading: Our Rooms & Spaces
rooms_section.subheading: Every room is designed for comfort and elegance, blending natural materials with modern amenities.

master_suite.name: Master Suite
master_suite.desc: A spacious king bedroom with en-suite bath, private terrace, and sweeping garden views.

bedroom_two.name: Bedroom Two
bedroom_two.desc: Twin beds that convert to a king, with a dedicated work nook and garden-facing window.

bedroom_three.name: Bedroom Three
bedroom_three.desc: A cosy queen room bathed in morning light, with built-in wardrobe and pool view.

living_dining.name: Living & Dining
living_dining.desc: An open-plan lounge and dining area that flows onto the pool terrace through folding glass doors.

kitchen.name: Kitchen
kitchen.desc: Fully equipped with a gas range, espresso machine, and everything you need for long, lazy breakfasts.

outdoor_bathroom.name: Outdoor Bathroom
outdoor_bathroom.desc: A semi-open bathroom with a deep soaking tub, rain shower, and tropical garden privacy wall.

location.heading: Location & Surroundings
location.description: Nestled in the lush hillside of Canggu, Bali — just minutes from the beach yet worlds away from the crowds. The perfect base for exploring the island's best surf breaks, rice terraces, and dining.

amenities.heading: Amenities & Facilities
amenities.subheading: Everything you need for a seamless stay — nothing you don't.

contact.heading: Get in Touch
contact.subheading: Ready to book or have a question? We'd love to hear from you — reach out and we'll get back to you within a few hours.

footer.tagline: A private sanctuary where luxury meets nature.

room_detail.cta_sub: Reach out and we'll take care of everything — from arrival to departure.

master_suite.title: Sleep in Elegance
master_suite.p1: The Master Suite is the villa's most spacious bedroom — a 45 m² retreat designed for complete rest. A hand-crafted king bed sits at the centre of the room, dressed in crisp Italian linen and surrounded by warm teak joinery and soft pendant lighting that shifts from morning brightness to amber evening warmth.
master_suite.p2: Sliding glass doors open directly onto your private terrace, where a pair of rattan chairs face the tropical garden. Completely shielded from the main pool area, it offers a level of quiet that is rare in villa stays — a place to linger over coffee at dawn or a nightcap long after the rest of the villa has gone to sleep.
master_suite.p3: The en-suite bathroom is finished in honed Javanese marble with a double vanity, deep soaking tub, and a frameless-glass rain shower. A full-length wardrobe runs the length of one wall, thoughtfully fitted with drawers, hanging space, and a hidden safe. Blackout curtains ensure that the room stays as dark as you need, for as long as you need.
master_suite.p4: An in-room espresso machine, a selection of teas, and a stocked minibar complete the picture — because the best mornings begin before you leave the room.

bedroom_two.title: A Room to Breathe
bedroom_two.p1: Bedroom Two is designed for flexibility and focus. Two single beds can be configured as a king at your request — ideal for couples or a pair of close friends who each want their own space. The room sits at the quieter end of the villa, shielded from the pool, and catches morning light through its garden-facing window from first light to midday.
bedroom_two.p2: A dedicated work nook with a solid timber desk and ergonomic chair makes this one of the few rooms in the villa suited to those who need to get something done. Fast WiFi reaches every corner, and the desk lamp is warm enough for late-night reading without disturbing a sleeping partner.
bedroom_two.p3: The en-suite shower room is compact but beautifully finished in natural stone, with a generous rain-head and quality toiletries. A built-in wardrobe and full-length mirror complete a room that gives you everything you need without excess.

bedroom_three.title: Morning Light, All Day
bedroom_three.about_heading: Small in Size, Generous in Light
bedroom_three.p1: Bedroom Three is the most intimate of the villa's bedrooms, and also its sunniest. A queen bed sits opposite a set of slatted timber shutters that frame the pool below — a view that rewards early risers with glittering morning light and rewards evening lingerers with the orange flicker of underwater pool lights in the dark.
bedroom_three.p2: The room is compact by design, giving it a warmth that larger rooms sometimes lack. Every surface has been chosen carefully — a timber headboard, hand-thrown ceramic lamps, and thick cotton curtains that diffuse the afternoon sun into something soft and golden.
bedroom_three.p3: The en-suite is neatly fitted with a rain shower, a stone basin, and a mirrored cabinet stocked with quality toiletries. A built-in wardrobe provides ample storage for a week-long stay, and the room is fully air-conditioned with individual temperature control.

living_dining.title: Where Everyone Gathers
living_dining.p1: The living and dining area is the spatial centrepiece of the villa — an 80 m² open-plan room that erases the boundary between indoors and out. Folding glass doors span the entire pool-facing wall and retract fully, turning the room into a covered outdoor pavilion when the weather invites it.
living_dining.p2: A long teak dining table seats twelve comfortably, making this the natural setting for slow group dinners, candle-lit evenings, or the kind of lazy breakfast that stretches to noon. The lounge area wraps around a pair of deep-cushioned sofas, a low coffee table, and floor-to-ceiling bookshelves stocked with novels, art books, and local maps.
living_dining.p3: A full sound system connects wirelessly to any device, and a large-format smart TV makes film nights an occasion. When you want something quieter, a set of day beds on the semi-covered terrace provide the perfect middle ground between inside and pool.

kitchen.title: Cook, Gather, Linger
kitchen.p1: Not all villa kitchens are created equal. This one was designed for guests who actually cook. A six-burner gas range sits beneath a stainless steel hood, flanked by generous prep surfaces in pale stone. Pots, pans, and knives are the proper kind — not the too-light versions that disappoint you the moment you pick them up.
kitchen.p2: The central island doubles as a breakfast bar, with three bar stools that fill up fast during morning coffee. An espresso machine, filtered water tap, and a dedicated juice bar make slow mornings effortless. The large-capacity fridge is stocked with essentials on arrival and has room for everything you might bring back from the local market.
kitchen.p3: A garden-facing window runs the length of the prep sink — the best view in the villa, some guests say — and everything from the dishwasher to the rice cooker is thoughtfully placed within reach. Whether you're cooking for two or feeding the whole villa, this kitchen handles it.

outdoor_bathroom.title: A Ritual, Not Just a Shower
outdoor_bathroom.p1: The outdoor bathroom is the villa's most unconventional space — and, for many guests, its most memorable. A deep stone soaking tub sits at the centre of a semi-open room, shielded from view by a living wall of bamboo and banana palms that filter the light and absorb the sounds of the outside world.
outdoor_bathroom.p2: A generous rain shower occupies one corner, fed by a wide overhead head that turns rinsing off into something worth staying under. The stone basin is paired with a timber shelf stocked with organic coconut-based toiletries — shampoo, conditioner, body wash, and a bar soap made locally. Nothing is in plastic.
outdoor_bathroom.p3: The space is designed to slow you down. Thick cotton towels and a folded bathrobe wait on the stone bench. A candle holder sits beside the tub. There is no speaker, no phone shelf, no rush. Just open sky, warm water, and the sound of leaves.
